channel.title: Influenza on México Pathogens Portal
channel.description: Recent content in Influenza on México Pathogens Portal

item.title: K-FluDB
item.description: Motivation: Influenza A viruses frequently cause seasonal outbreaks and pandemics due to their genetic diversity and reassortment potential. Existing genomic surveillance tools face challenges with redundant databases, delaying subtype identification and obscuring reassortment dynamics. K-FluDB, a novel k-mer-based database, addresses these issues by enhancing subtype identification, capturing genomic diversity, and assisting in the detection of reassortment events critical for understanding viral evolution and improving outbreak proactive measures.

item.title: Influenza
item.description: Offers insights into influenza activity across Mexico, highlighting weekly case trends, seasonal peaks, subtype circulation, and year-to-year variation. The objective is to enhance national surveillance, anticipate transmission dynamics, and support timely public health responses.
